title: Technology
description: Modern tech vocabulary for digital communication

vocabulary.meaning: The amount of data a network can transmit
vocabulary.example: We need more bandwidth for video calls.

vocabulary.meaning: A newer version of software or an app
vocabulary.example: Please install the latest security update.

vocabulary.meaning: A secret word used to access an account
vocabulary.example: Use a strong password with letters and numbers.

vocabulary.meaning: To copy a file from the internet to your device
vocabulary.example: Download the app from the App Store.

vocabulary.meaning: To send a file from your device to the internet
vocabulary.example: Please upload the document to Google Drive.

vocabulary.meaning: A message alert from an app
vocabulary.example: Turn off notifications during meetings.

vocabulary.meaning: A captured image of your screen
vocabulary.example: Take a screenshot and send it to me.

vocabulary.meaning: A copy of data stored for safety
vocabulary.example: Always backup your important files.

vocabulary.meaning: To find and fix problems in a system
vocabulary.example: Let me troubleshoot this connection issue.

vocabulary.meaning: Storing data on internet servers
vocabulary.example: My photos are saved in cloud storage.

vocabulary.meaning: A mobile phone with internet and computer capabilities
vocabulary.example: Most people carry a smartphone these days.

vocabulary.meaning: A portable personal computer
vocabulary.example: I work on my laptop at home.

vocabulary.meaning: A portable touchscreen computer
vocabulary.example: Kids love using a tablet for learning.

vocabulary.meaning: The main chip in a computer that runs programs
vocabulary.example: A fast processor improves performance.

vocabulary.meaning: Random Access Memory — temporary storage for running programs
vocabulary.example: Adding more RAM will speed up your computer.

vocabulary.meaning: The capacity to save files and data on a device
vocabulary.example: I need more storage for my photos.

vocabulary.meaning: A device used to recharge a battery
vocabulary.example: Don't forget your phone charger.

vocabulary.meaning: Connecting to a network without physical cables
vocabulary.example: The printer is wireless — no cables needed.

vocabulary.meaning: A short-range wireless technology for connecting devices
vocabulary.example: Pair your headphones via Bluetooth.

vocabulary.meaning: Wireless internet connection
vocabulary.example: Connect to the wifi network in the office.

vocabulary.meaning: A location offering wifi access or a phone used as a router
vocabulary.example: Use your phone as a hotspot.

vocabulary.meaning: To watch or listen to media online without downloading
vocabulary.example: I stream music on Spotify every day.

vocabulary.meaning: A pause in streaming when data is loading
vocabulary.example: The video keeps buffering due to slow internet.

vocabulary.meaning: A name used to identify yourself on a system
vocabulary.example: Enter your username and password to log in.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of entering your credentials to access a system
vocabulary.example: Click the login button to access your account.

vocabulary.meaning: The right to control who sees your personal information
vocabulary.example: Review your privacy settings regularly.

vocabulary.meaning: Security software that blocks unauthorized network access
vocabulary.example: The firewall blocked the malicious traffic.

vocabulary.meaning: Software designed to detect and remove malware
vocabulary.example: Install antivirus software on your computer.

vocabulary.meaning: Software designed to damage or gain unauthorized access
vocabulary.example: The computer was infected with malware.

vocabulary.meaning: A scam to steal personal data via fake emails
vocabulary.example: Beware of phishing emails from unknown senders.

vocabulary.meaning: Converting data into a coded format to prevent unauthorized access
vocabulary.example: Your messages are protected by encryption.

vocabulary.meaning: A security process requiring two forms of verification
vocabulary.example: Enable two-factor authentication for added security.

vocabulary.meaning: A software application for a phone or computer
vocabulary.example: Download the banking app from the store.

vocabulary.meaning: Programs and operating systems used by a computer
vocabulary.example: The software needs to be updated.

vocabulary.meaning: The physical components of a computer
vocabulary.example: The hardware upgrade improved speed significantly.

vocabulary.meaning: The main software that manages a computer's resources
vocabulary.example: Windows and macOS are popular operating systems.

vocabulary.meaning: A program used to access the internet
vocabulary.example: Chrome is a popular web browser.

vocabulary.meaning: A website used to search for information online
vocabulary.example: Google is the most used search engine.

vocabulary.meaning: To set up a program on a device
vocabulary.example: Install the update to fix the bug.

vocabulary.meaning: Replacing something with a newer or better version
vocabulary.example: I need to upgrade my laptop's memory.

vocabulary.meaning: To find and fix errors in a computer program
vocabulary.example: The developer spent hours debugging the code.

vocabulary.meaning: Temporary data stored for faster access
vocabulary.example: Clear the cache to free up space.

vocabulary.meaning: A computer that provides data to other computers
vocabulary.example: The server went down for maintenance.

vocabulary.meaning: Information processed or stored by a computer
vocabulary.example: Back up your data regularly.

vocabulary.meaning: A set of rules or steps used to solve a problem
vocabulary.example: Social media feeds use an algorithm.

vocabulary.meaning: The part of software you see and interact with
vocabulary.example: The app has a simple and clean interface.

vocabulary.meaning: An object in orbit used for communications and GPS
vocabulary.example: GPS relies on satellite signals.

vocabulary.meaning: Computer systems that simulate human intelligence
vocabulary.example: Artificial intelligence is changing many industries.

vocabulary.meaning: A computer-generated simulated environment
vocabulary.example: Virtual reality headsets are becoming popular.

vocabulary.meaning: The practice of protecting systems from digital attacks
vocabulary.example: Cybersecurity is a top priority for all companies.

vocabulary.meaning: A device that directs internet traffic between networks
vocabulary.example: Restart the router if the internet is slow.

vocabulary.meaning: A device that connects your home network to the internet
vocabulary.example: The modem converts signals from your internet provider.

vocabulary.meaning: The smallest unit of a digital image or display
vocabulary.example: This screen has a resolution of 1920 by 1080 pixels.

vocabulary.meaning: The clarity of an image measured in pixels
vocabulary.example: High resolution makes photos look sharp and clear.

vocabulary.meaning: A display that responds to touch input
vocabulary.example: Most smartphones have a touchscreen display.

vocabulary.meaning: To make data the same across multiple devices
vocabulary.example: Sync your contacts between your phone and laptop.

vocabulary.meaning: When a computer or program suddenly stops working
vocabulary.example: The application crashed and I lost my work.

vocabulary.meaning: To restart a computer or device
vocabulary.example: Try rebooting your computer to fix the issue.

vocabulary.meaning: A delay in response time on a computer or network
vocabulary.example: There's too much lag in this online game.

vocabulary.meaning: A digital audio series available for streaming or download
vocabulary.translation: chuong trinh phat thanh truc tuyen
vocabulary.example: I listen to a technology podcast every morning.

vocabulary.meaning: A small electronic device worn on the body
vocabulary.example: Smartwatches are popular wearable devices.

vocabulary.meaning: An unmanned flying device controlled remotely
vocabulary.example: Drones are used for aerial photography.

vocabulary.meaning: Technology that overlays digital content on the real world
vocabulary.example: Augmented reality apps let you preview furniture in your room.

vocabulary.meaning: Using body measurements like fingerprints for identification
vocabulary.example: Biometric scanning unlocks your phone with your fingerprint.

vocabulary.meaning: Using remote servers on the internet to process and store data
vocabulary.example: Cloud computing lets businesses scale without buying servers.

vocabulary.meaning: A decentralized digital ledger for recording transactions
vocabulary.example: Blockchain technology is the foundation of cryptocurrency.

vocabulary.meaning: Using technology to perform tasks without human input
vocabulary.example: Automation has improved factory efficiency.

vocabulary.meaning: Intentionally slowing down internet speed by a provider
vocabulary.translation: giam toc do mang co y
vocabulary.example: Some providers use bandwidth throttling during peak hours.

vocabulary.meaning: The trail of data you leave behind when using the internet
vocabulary.example: Be careful about your digital footprint on social media.

vocabulary.meaning: A home equipped with devices that can be controlled remotely
vocabulary.example: Our smart home lets us control the lights with our voice.

vocabulary.meaning: A network of physical objects connected to the internet
vocabulary.example: The internet of things connects everyday devices online.

vocabulary.meaning: Buying and selling goods online
vocabulary.example: E-commerce has grown rapidly in recent years.

vocabulary.meaning: Synthetic media created using AI to imitate real people
vocabulary.example: Deepfake technology raises concerns about misinformation.

vocabulary.meaning: Software whose source code is freely available to anyone
vocabulary.example: Linux is a popular open source operating system.

vocabulary.meaning: The time delay between sending and receiving data
vocabulary.example: Low latency is important for video conferencing.

vocabulary.meaning: An external device connected to a computer such as a mouse or keyboard
vocabulary.example: A monitor is a common computer peripheral.

vocabulary.meaning: Able to work together with another device or software
vocabulary.example: Make sure the charger is compatible with your phone.

vocabulary.meaning: Unwanted messages sent in bulk, usually by email
vocabulary.example: My inbox is full of spam emails.

vocabulary.meaning: A small file stored by a website on your computer
vocabulary.example: This website uses cookies to remember your preferences.

vocabulary.meaning: Virtual Private Network — a secure connection over the internet
vocabulary.example: Use a VPN to protect your data on public wifi.

vocabulary.meaning: The address name of a website on the internet
vocabulary.example: We registered a new domain for our business website.

vocabulary.meaning: An early model of a product built for testing
vocabulary.example: The company showed a prototype of the new phone.

vocabulary.meaning: A small, temporary fault in a system or device
vocabulary.example: There was a glitch in the payment system this morning.

vocabulary.meaning: AI that can create text, images, code, or audio from prompts
vocabulary.example: ChatGPT is a popular generative AI tool.

vocabulary.meaning: An AI trained on vast amounts of text to understand and generate language
vocabulary.example: GPT-4 is a large language model from OpenAI.

vocabulary.meaning: Processing data close to where it is generated rather than in a central cloud
vocabulary.example: Edge computing reduces latency for IoT devices.

vocabulary.meaning: Computers using quantum phenomena to perform calculations
vocabulary.example: Quantum computing could break current encryption methods.

vocabulary.meaning: The fifth generation of mobile network technology
vocabulary.example: 5G enables download speeds many times faster than 4G.

vocabulary.meaning: An immersive virtual shared space combining physical and digital worlds
vocabulary.example: Companies are building workspaces in the metaverse.

vocabulary.meaning: Technology overlaying digital information onto the real world
vocabulary.example: AR apps let you see furniture in your room before buying.

vocabulary.meaning: A computer-generated immersive three-dimensional environment
vocabulary.example: Virtual reality headsets provide immersive gaming experiences.

vocabulary.meaning: A virtual replica of a physical object or system
vocabulary.example: Engineers use digital twins to simulate factory operations.

vocabulary.meaning: Unique physical characteristics used to identify individuals
vocabulary.example: Biometrics like fingerprints are used for secure login.

vocabulary.meaning: A home with internet-connected devices that can be controlled remotely
vocabulary.example: A smart home system controls lights, heating and security.

vocabulary.meaning: Software with code freely available for anyone to use or modify
vocabulary.example: Linux is the most famous open source operating system.

vocabulary.meaning: A platform allowing non-programmers to build apps without writing code
vocabulary.translation: nền tảng không cần lập trình
vocabulary.example: No-code tools let businesses build apps without developers.

vocabulary.meaning: A security process requiring two forms of identification
vocabulary.example: Enable two-factor authentication for better account security.

vocabulary.meaning: A software flaw unknown to the developer, exploitable by attackers
vocabulary.example: The zero-day vulnerability was patched within hours.

vocabulary.meaning: The set of technologies used to build an application
vocabulary.example: Our tech stack uses React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL.

vocabulary.meaning: Minimum Viable Product — a basic version of a product to test demand
vocabulary.translation: sản phẩm khả dụng tối thiểu (MVP)
vocabulary.example: Launch an MVP first to get real user feedback.

quiz.question: What do you do to get an app from the internet?
What is a captured image of your screen?
What is a copy of data stored for safety?
What is used to protect your account?
What do you do when fixing system problems?
What does 'sync' mean?
options: To make data the same across devices

quiz.question: What is a 'router'?
options: A device that directs internet traffic

quiz.question: What is 'augmented reality'?
options: Technology overlaying digital content on the real world

quiz.question: What is a 'digital footprint'?
options: The trail of data you leave online
